Our mission is simple (but not easy): make complex work easier, smoother, and more intuitive for our operators, while keeping our infrastructure scalable and easy to build on.
We focus on creating a consistent, thoughtful experience across internal tools and connected SaaS systems, helping finance operators move seamlessly between products every day. Alongside mature platforms, we’re also building new workflow and task management experiences, with a growing investment in AI-powered capabilities to support smarter decision-making.

The Music Mission team owns Spotify’s end to end proposition for music creators and the experiences they create for fans. The team is dedicated to building tools and services to enable creation, promotion, expression, and monetization at scale. The Music Mission team is responsible for shaping how music is created, managed, and experienced on Spotify. Within this, the Rights Management team builds products that help rights holders seamlessly interact with Spotify’s platform. You’ll join a cross-functional group spanning R&D, Partnerships, Legal, and Operations, working together to make rights management more scalable, transparent, and effective for creators and their teams.

description:
Spotify’s mission is to unlock the potential of human creativity by giving a million creative artists the opportunity to live off their art and billions of fans the chance to enjoy and be passionate about these creators.
 
The Subscriptions Mission builds and evolves Spotify’s subscription products and marketplace experiences to drive sustainable user and revenue growth globally. We focus on awareness, acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization strategies that help users unlock the full value of Spotify while enabling the business to scale efficiently and responsibly.
 
User Understanding sits within Subscriptions and focuses on building the intelligence layer that makes our growth efforts smarter—through decisioning, data signals, and ML models that power personalization across surfaces and lifecycle moments. You’ll work at the intersection of machine learning innovation and commercial strategy, partnering closely with ML engineers, data scientists, data platform teams, product insights, and business stakeholders to shape high-impact growth initiatives.

The Personalization team makes deciding what to play next easier and more enjoyable for every listener. From Blend to Discover Weekly, we’re behind some of Spotify’s most-loved features. We built them by understanding the world of music and podcasts better than anyone else. Join us and you’ll keep millions of users listening by making great recommendations to each and every one of them.
 Spotify is building the future of music, podcasts, and audio experiences — and conversational AI is a key part of that journey. Within our Conversation Product Area, we’re creating the platform that powers intelligent, interactive experiences across Spotify. 
In this role, you’ll lead the Agent Platform — the foundation behind experiences like AI DJ, Prompted Playlists, and integrations with partners such as ChatGPT, Google Gemini, and Alexa. You’ll help bring these systems together into a unified platform that enables teams across Spotify to build consistent, high-quality conversational experiences.
compensation_raw:

The benefits available for this position include health insurance, six-month paid parental leave, 401(k) retirement plan, monthly meal allowance, 23 paid days off, paid flexible holidays, and paid sick leave. These ranges may be modified in the future.

description:
We design Spotify’s consumer experience—end to end, moment to moment, across every screen, platform, and partner integration. Our mission is to make listening feel effortless, personal, and joyful for billions of users around the world. That means turning complexity into clarity across hundreds of touchpoints—from our mobile and desktop apps to the smart speakers, TVs, cars, and integrations where Spotify shows up every day. If it touches a consumer, we shape it. We bring deep insight into human behavior, design, and technology to craft experiences that feel intuitive, expressive, and unmistakably Spotify.
We’re seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the playback experience across Spotify’s mobile apps. This is one of the most highly used parts of the product, where millions of users engage with music, podcasts, audiobooks, and video every day. You’ll shape how content, controls, and context come together to create seamless, personalized listening experiences in real time.

About EliseAI At EliseAI, we're improving the industries that matter most: housing and healthcare. Everyone needs a place to live and access to quality healthcare, yet both are often harder to secure than they should be. By integrating AI agents deeply into existing workflows, we make them more efficient, reduce costs, and improve the experience for everyone. Housing : We simplify how renters tour apartments, sign leases, submit maintenance requests, and stay connected with their property team—bringing everything they need for their home into one place. Healthcare : We make it easy to schedule appointments, complete intake forms, and we help patients communicate with providers, so everyone can focus on health instead of paperwork. With EliseAI, organizations reduce manual work, improve accessibility, and deliver a seamless experience across essential services. We recently raised a $250 million Series E round led by Andreessen Horowitz to accelerate this mission. About The Role EliseAI is rapidly expanding into new product lines serving large, sophisticated customers, and we are investing heavily in how those products are built, launched, and scaled. The Product Operations Manager plays a central role in shaping this next phase of growth. You will work at the heart of product development, partnering closely with Product and Engineering to turn ambitious ideas into reliable, enterprise-ready solutions. This role offers meaningful ownership over execution, launch quality, and iteration speed as we scale a fast-growing business line. You will operate close to both the product surface area and the underlying data, using technical and analytical skills to improve quality, adoption, and speed. Key Responsibilities Translate product requirements into clear, buildable workflows through specs, diagrams, and edge-case definition Conduct thorough discovery sessions with new clients to understand their needs and requirements Drive launch readiness and coordinate cross-functional stakeholders through release Own enterprise implementation readiness, including migrations, permissions, templates, and configurations Lead post-launch follow-through to ensure issues are identified, resolved, and learned from Use SQL and internal tools to analyze product usage, adoption, and workflow health Own feedback loop conversations, synthesizing data across multiple channels such as call snippets, Datadog, support tickets, surveys, and more—acting as the Voice of the Customer to drive short- and long-term improvements Build and scale product operations processes, including monitoring, issue resolution, and ongoing support operations Manage product compliance requirements and collaborate with Legal on regulatory or policy-related initiatives Attract top-tier talent to join our driven team Move at rocket speed, build something massive. We’re scaling fast, solving real client problems with precision and ambition. Here, you own your impact; full autonomy, no micromanagement, no fluff. We hire the best, expect the best, and give you the masterclass of your career. It’s hard, it’s intense, and it’s the most rewarding work you’ll ever do. If you’re hungry, driven, and ready to build something massive, climb aboard. Requirements Experience in product operations, technical program management, or execution-focused roles at a high-growth technology company Working proficiency in SQL and comfort driving independent analysis Proven ability to manage complex, cross-functional initiatives in ambiguous environments Strong written communication skills, including specs and process documentation High attention to detail with strong ownership and follow-through Willingness to work in person at our office 4-5 days a week Why Join Growth and impact. It’s not often that you can get in on the ground floor of a funded ( unicorn !) startup that’s scaling so fast. That means that instead of following a playbook, you’ll be writing it. Every single day you will be challenged to identify how we can scale and execute on it. You’ll learn what works when you succeed and what doesn’t when you fail. Either way, the rest of the team will be here to support you. Benefits In addition to the growth and impact you’ll have at EliseAI, we offer competitive salaries along with the following benefits: Equity in the company Medical, Dental and Vision premiums covered at 100% Fully paid parental leave Commuter benefits 401k benefits Fitness & home services stipend to cover part of your expenses so you can focus on what matters A collaborative in-office environment with an open floor plan, fully stocked kitchen, and all meals covered in the office Unlimited vacation and paid holidays We'll cover relocation packages and make the move exciting, not painful! Job Compensation Range The salary range for this role is $150,000 - $220,000. EliseAI offers a competitive total rewards package which includes base salary, equity, and a comprehensive benefits & perks package. ABOUT BASETEN Baseten powers mission-critical inference for the world's most dynamic AI companies, like Cursor, Notion, OpenEvidence, Abridge, Clay, Gamma and Writer. By uniting applied AI research, flexible infrastructure, and seamless developer tooling, we enable companies operating at the frontier of AI to bring cutting-edge models into production. Join us and help build the platform engineers turn to to ship AI products. THE ROLE As a Core Product Manager at Baseten, you’ll shape the core developer experience that powers how users build, deploy, and manage AI applications on our platform. You’ll drive the roadmap for user-facing APIs, SDKs, UI workflows, and integration surfaces, turning complex ML infrastructure into a seamless, intuitive product. Your work will directly impact how teams interact with Baseten day-to-day, ensuring that our platform is both powerful and a joy to use. EXAMPLE INITIATIVES Chains for multi-component workflows Asynchronous inference Model APIs for frontier models Model training built for production inference RESPONSIBILITIES Own the roadmap for Baseten’s core product surface, including APIs, UI, and developer workflows Partner with design and engineering to deliver elegant, intuitive developer experiences Collaborate with customers to deeply understand pain points and define solutions that simplify AI deployment Drive new product launches and improve onboarding, observability, and management experiences Align product initiatives with company strategy and ensure measurable product adoption and retention REQUIREMENTS 4+ years of Product Management experience in developer tools, SaaS, or ML platforms Engineering background (e.g., degree in Computer Science, Electrical Engineering, or related field; or equivalent hands-on experience as a software engineer) Deep empathy for developers and an ability to translate technical complexity into simple, usable products Experience driving cross-functional alignment across design, engineering, and GTM teams Proven ability to define product success metrics and iterate based on data and feedback NICE TO HAVE Familiarity with AI/ML workflows and inference products Experience building products for technical users (e.g., APIs, SDKs, dashboards) Background in developer experience or product-led growth BENEFITS Competitive compensation, including meaningful equity. 100% coverage of medical, dental, and vision insurance for employee and dependents Flexible PTO policy including company wide Winter Break (our offices are closed from Christmas Eve to New Year's Day!) Paid parental leave Fertility and family-building stipend through Carrot Company-facilitated 401(k) Exposure to a variety of ML startups, offering unparalleled learning and networking opportunities. Intercom is the AI Customer Service company on a mission to help businesses provide incredible customer experiences. Our AI agent Fin, the most advanced customer service AI agent on the market, lets businesses deliver always-on, impeccable customer service and ultimately transform their customer experiences for the better. Fin can also be combined with our Helpdesk to become a complete solution called the Intercom Customer Service Suite, which provides AI enhanced support for the more complex or high touch queries that require a human agent. Founded in 2011 and trusted by nearly 30,000 global businesses, Intercom is setting the new standard for customer service. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and consistently deliver incredible value to our customers. We’re looking for a Product Manager to join our team and help shape the future of business messaging and AI-powered customer engagement. This is a career-defining opportunity to lead a world-class team of engineers, designers, and researchers to deliver products that touch millions of people every day. What will I be doing? Be responsible for a significant product area or help lead an important project. Deeply understand the customer problems you’re tackling through research, data, and direct customer interactions — then frame those problems clearly for your team. Manage the roadmap; defining what should be built, when and why - and communicate it to generate alignment and excitement. Collaborate with engineers and designers to imagine, build, and ship solutions, from bold future visions to small, focused iterations. Leverage AI to increase your own efficiency, accelerate product development, and multiply your team’s impact. Evaluate impact through quantitative and qualitative measures, both in beta and after full release. Own and maintain the quality of your product area, ensuring every release delivers world-class software. Collaborate with peers across the business to ensure we deliver products with clear GTM strategy and execution, getting hands on wherever necessary to ensure we deliver. Be a leader in your team or projects by role-modelling our company values and R&D principles. What skills do I need? There is no minimum years of experience for this role. We’re interested in your impact, drive, and potential. Instead of years, we’ll be looking for evidence that you’ve applied and grown these skills: Sharp Customer Focus & Problem Framing - able to independently understand, define and articulate complex customer problems and opportunities using data analysis and qualitative inputs. Compelling Roadmaps - define the roadmap of features, products or areas of focus for your given area, communicating what, when and why to generate excitement with customers and the team Excellent Product Judgment - skilled at balancing customer needs, business impact, and technical constraints to define, advocate for, and deliver the right product. Build World-Class Product - able to deeply collaborate with engineering and designers to quickly ship effective solutions. But, also able to independently accelerate the process leveraging AI. AI Tech - Able to understand AI systems and LLMs, assess feasibility and trade-offs, and make informed product decisions in close collaboration with engineering. Technical fluency - Comfortable engaging with engineers and customers on the modern tech stack — from APIs and SaaS infrastructure to low-code tooling and system architecture. Outcome Oriented - define, fight for and share progress against the customer and business impact of your product area or project. Leadership - Excellent communicator. High ownership. Ability to influence and drive alignment. Benefits We are a well treated bunch, with awesome benefits! If there’s something important to you that’s not on this list, talk to us! Competitive salary and equity in a fast-growing start-up We serve lunch every weekday, plus a variety of snack foods and a fully stocked kitchen Regular compensation reviews - we reward great work! Pension scheme & match up to 4% Peace of mind with life assurance, as well as comprehensive health and dental insurance for you and your dependents Flexible paid time off policy Paid maternity leave, as well as 6 weeks paternity leave for fathers, to let you spend valuable time with your loved ones If you’re cycling, we’ve got you covered on the Cycle-to-Work Scheme. With secure bike storage too MacBooks are our standard, but we also offer Windows for certain roles when needed. Policies Intercom has a hybrid working policy. We believe that working in person helps us stay connected, collaborate easier and create a great culture while still providing flexibility to work from home. We expect employees to be in the office at least three days per week. We have a radically open and accepting culture at Intercom. We avoid spending time on divisive subjects to foster a safe and cohesive work environment for everyone. As an organization, our policy is to not advocate on behalf of the company or our employees on any social or political topics out of our internal or external communications. We respect personal opinion and expression on these topics on personal social platforms on personal time, and do not challenge or confront anyone for their views on non-work related topics. Our goal is to focus on doing incredible work to achieve our goals and unite the company through our core values . Intercom values diversity and is committed to a policy of Equal Employment Opportunity. Intercom will not discriminate against an applicant or employee on the basis of race, color, religion, creed, national origin, ancestry, sex, gender, age, physical or mental disability, veteran or military status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, marital status, or any other legally recognized protected basis under federal, state, or local law.

Fin can also be combined with our Helpdesk to become a complete solution called the Intercom Customer Service Suite, which provides AI enhanced support for the more complex or high touch queries that require a human agent. Founded in 2011 and trusted by nearly 30,000 global businesses, Intercom is setting the new standard for customer service. Driven by our core values, we push boundaries, build with speed and intensity, and consistently deliver incredible value to our customers. Intercom is hiring an exceptional Staff/Principal level Product Designer to push the boundaries of product craft. We are one of the most design-driven and AI first technology companies. Fin, our AI Agent is the best performing, most widely adopted, and highest grossing AI service agent on the market. We are shaping the future of how businesses communicate with their customers through AI and humans. Our product is used by millions, and our design team plays a critical role in making it world-class. Why you should join Intercom today AI is transforming everything—fast. We’re at the forefront, shaping the AI Agent category and redefining customer service with Fin. Soon, Fin will handle the vast majority of customer interactions, setting a new standard for support. This shift isn’t just changing what we build—it’s changing how we work. Our team is constantly evolving, integrating AI into design, development, and go-to-market strategies. At Intercom, design is in our DNA. With 60+ designers across R&D and Brand and two co-founders from design, you’ll never have to fight for your place at the table. Here, designers lead from the start, shaping products that millions of people interact with daily. If you want to work on cutting-edge AI experiences that push the boundaries of product design, there’s no better place to be. Why this role exists In a world where it’s easier and easier to get software off the ground and to 75% built, it’s the last 25% that really matters. We need deep craft specialists—people who will raise the quality bar, bring a spark of creativity, drive best-in-class interaction and visual design, and push execution excellence across our most strategic product areas. This is not a strategy-only or people-management role. We need a maker, a doer, a leader in craft. If you have a deep passion for interaction design, product thinking, and pixel-perfect execution—and the leadership and autonomy to drive impact without waiting for direction—this is the role for you. Must Have Experience We’re looking for a world-class IC designer who: Has shipped complex, high-quality software products. Designs for multiple surfaces—desktop, mobile, chat interfaces, AI first and emerging experiences. Balances speed and detail—knows when to move fast and when to obsess over the last 1%. Thinks end-to-end—from big-picture systems thinking to the smallest interaction detail. Understands engineering and can build prototypes to communicate ideas (Framer, Loveable, Windsurf, Vercel, Figma, code etc...). Has taste. You know what great design looks and feels like, and you push to get there. Can tell a compelling story—articulating your decisions with clarity and conviction. Operates with agency, ownership, and autonomy—doesn’t wait to be told what to do. Who This Role Is Not For This role is not for you if: ● You prefer strategy, research or system design over execution—this is a craft-heavy role. ● You need a lot of direction or structured processes—you should thrive in open-endedness and ambiguity. ● You don’t obsess over details—we’re looking for someone who sweats the pixels. ● You primarily work on marketing sites—this role is about designing deep, functional software experiences. Day-to-Day Responsibilities What you’ll be doing: ● Owning end-to-end design for high-impact product areas from concept to execution. ● Defining new interaction patterns and design paradigms for Fin, our automation, and AI-powered tools. ● Sketching, prototyping, and testing ideas at different fidelities to validate decisions. ● Working closely with engineers and PMs to define problems, move quickly, and deliver high-quality solutions. ● Maintaining high standards of craft while balancing speed and execution. ● Contributing to design culture—mentoring others, pushing for greatness in a team, refining processes, and sharing knowledge inside and outside Intercom. What Success Looks Like You’ll know you’re successful if: 1. You’ve raised the design bar. Your work is referenced as an example of exceptional craft and taste. 2. You’ve shipped product with major business impact. The areas you own are visibly better because of you. 3. You operate autonomously. You don’t need direction—you see what needs to be done and do it. 4. Engineers love working with you. Your designs are considered, clear, and easy to implement. 5. Your peers trust your judgment. When you push for quality, people listen. Interview Process We understand that interviewing for a new role is both time and energy consuming–we aim to be thorough, speedy and transparent in our approach. You should expect for the entire process to be no more than 4 weeks from start to finish. You’ll meet with various members of our team and have the opportunity to fully validate that Intercom and the role is the right fit for you. Ready to build something great? If you're driven to push the boundaries of product design and have the craft, autonomy, and taste to make a real impact, we want to hear from you. With powerful AI Agents embedded across our platform, teams can analyze, test, and optimize user experiences faster than ever. Ranked #1 across multiple categories in G2’s Winter 2026 Report, Amplitude is the best-in-class solution for product, data, and marketing teams. Learn more at amplitude.com . We operate from a place of humility, take ownership of problems and successes, approach challenges with a growth mindset, and put our customers at the center of everything we do. We strive to create an environment of inclusion—one focused on psychological safety, empathy, and human connection—that will allow employees of all backgrounds to thrive. About the Role We’re building a People team powered by AI — not just augmented by it. This new role embeds an AI practitioner directly inside the People & Places function to design, deploy, and scale AI agents that take on high-volume tactical work, freeing the team to focus on what only humans can do. This is not a traditional PM role: there’s no external roadmap, no feature backlog. You’ll partner across Talent, Total Rewards, HRBPs, L&D, and People Ops to find the highest-impact opportunities, then build and ship them. Serve as the People team’s strategic AI advisor — translating market possibilities into practical, impactful solutions. What We’re Looking For 5+ years in product management, with meaningful experience inside or directly supporting an HR / People Operations environment. Hands-on experience scoping and deploying AI or automation in HR contexts (onboarding agents, self-service bots, compensation tools, etc.). Familiarity with modern HR tech stacks (ATS, HRIS, LMS) and their native AI capabilities. Experience with AI/LLM tools — no-code platforms, API-based solutions, or prompt-driven agents — and translating them into real workflows. Able to write real code (Python, JavaScript, or similar) — not just prompt your way through a problem. Strong ability to earn trust with non-technical stakeholders and translate operational pain into clear product requirements. Bias for action, comfort with ambiguity, and good judgment about where AI should and shouldn’t operate in people processes. Built conversational AI agents for HR use cases (onboarding assistants, policy Q&A bots, manager coaching tools). Experience with agentic frameworks — n8n, Zapier, LangChain, Claude, or similar. Mixpanel delivers insights that customers trust. Visit mixpanel.com to learn more. We operate from first principles, challenge assumptions, and bias toward simple, elegant solutions. Product owns execution end to end. The Product Platform team’s mission is to make Mixpanel easy to manage, secure, compliant, and built on a reliable foundation. The team runs two engineering tracks: a server infrastructure and application security track, and an enterprise management and UI track. This isn’t a role about a single feature or workflow — it’s about the foundational systems that determine whether a 500-person company trusts us with their data and their organizational complexity. In the last six months AI agents have crossed a meaningful threshold, evolving from turn-by-turn assistance to autonomous systems that reason, plan, and execute complex work without intervention. Intelligence is no longer the bottleneck. Connecting it to real organizational data and workflows is the hard part, and the companies that build the right infrastructure now, while the stack is still being defined, will be difficult to displace. Airtable is building the platform for human-agent collaboration: a shared operational surface where humans and agents work together on the same data, with the same visibility and the same rules. Omni is the agent at the center of that vision. It lets anyone build apps, automate workflows, and get answers from their operational data through natural language. It's the primary surface through which customers experience AI in Airtable, and the vehicle through which we make good on the promise that Airtable becomes more valuable as AI proliferates. Think of Omni as a forward-deployed Airtable engineer in every base that understands your data and helps turn anyone into a builder. This role owns the full Omni experience: the build loop (turning intent into working apps and workflows) and the intelligence layer (analysis, Q&A, and synthesis over operational data). You'll be responsible for making Omni the best way for business users to build and operate AI-powered apps and workflows. Why this matters now: AI is reshaping how software gets built and used. Tools like Lovable, Replit, and Cursor have shown that AI can build real applications, but none of them solve for structured data, collaboration, or enterprise trust. That's the gap Omni is built to own. The scope is broad. This role spans the full AI experience in Airtable, from building apps and workflows to configuring agents to analyzing data. You own the primary way customers interact with AI across the product. Enterprise customers are ahead of us. Our largest accounts are already running AI-powered workflows and pushing us on what's next. The demand is specific, urgent, and shapes our roadmap directly. Massive distribution. Half the Fortune 100 are customers and we have hundreds of thousands of monthly self-serve signups. Omni improvements reach enormous scale immediately. What you'll do Own the end-to-end Omni experience. From the moment a user describes what they need, through app creation, workflow configuration, and ongoing iteration. Omni should feel like an expert consultant, not a chatbot. Push the frontier of what Omni can do. Partner closely with engineering to evolve Omni's agent architecture, define how it reasons about and executes multi-step plans, and ensure it compounds with model improvements rather than requiring piecemeal capability additions. As models get smarter, Omni should get smarter automatically. Make app building dramatically better. Raise the quality ceiling on what Omni can build: richer interfaces, more sophisticated automations, better data modeling. We should be the best way to build collaborative operational apps. Make it easy to start from real data. Most people don't build from a blank canvas. They have messy spreadsheets, file exports, or data living in a warehouse. You'll own the experience of going from "here's what I have" to "here's a working app," making Omni the fastest path from raw data to something useful. Define and drive adoption metrics. Establish north star metrics for Omni that capture whether we're making people more successful, build a measurement framework, and use it to drive roadmap prioritization. Collaborate across teams. Work closely with the teams that own agents, MCP, integrations, enterprise, and self-serve to ensure Omni is coherent across all surfaces. Who you are Experience shipping AI agent or copilot products. You've built products where an LLM reasons, plans, and takes action, not just generates text. You understand agent loops, tool use, context management, and the difference between a demo and a production-quality agent experience. 8+ years of product management experience with a track record of shipping products users love, ideally including AI-powered features at scale. Deep AI technical fluency. You understand how LLMs work at a technical level, not just as a user. You can evaluate tradeoffs between model capabilities, have opinions on agent architectures, evals, prompt design, and know what it means to build so that your product gets better as models get better. Strong product taste for AI-native building experiences. You have a refined sense for what makes an AI building experience feel magical vs. frustrating. You use AI tools (Claude Code, Cursor, Lovable, etc.) to build and prototype, not just spec. Analytical rigor. You can define success metrics for inherently fuzzy AI outcomes, build measurement frameworks, and use data to distinguish real quality improvements from noise. description: Replit is the agentic software creation platform that enables anyone to build applications using natural language. With millions of users worldwide, Replit is democratizing software development by removing traditional barriers to application creation. About the Role We're looking for an AI-native product growth lead to own Replit's entire paid acquisition and lifecycle marketing engine as the single DRI. This is an IC role for someone who builds systems, not just campaigns. Someone who uses AI and automation aggressively to do what traditionally requires an entire team. You don't need an engineering background. You need the instinct to build: when you see a repetitive task, you reach for Replit or an API before you reach for a spreadsheet. You'll have data science and data engineering partners for infrastructure and modeling, and brand marketing will set creative direction. Your job is to turn that direction into a closed-loop optimization system: a self-improving system that turns performance data into better creative, optimizes spend across channels in near-real-time, and compounds every insight so nothing learned is ever lost. Each cycle, the system gets smarter. That's the engine you'll build and own. The ideal candidate has deep channel expertise across paid search, paid social, and lifecycle, but their real edge is building AI-powered workflows that scale creative production, automate measurement, and compound institutional knowledge. You Will Own full-funnel performance marketing across paid search & social, ASO, and lifecycle Build a self-improving creative engine: AI-driven ad generation, testing, and iteration that scales without scaling headcount Maintain a persistent knowledge layer so every experiment, result, and creative insight compounds automatically into the next cycle Own conversion signal quality end-to-end: right events, right audiences, right attribution, partnering with DE on the infrastructure Run a structured experimentation program where every test feeds a feedback loop, not a one-off report Automate performance reporting and alerting — your system flags problems (creative fatigue, CAC shifts, payback drift) before anyone has to ask Build geo-differentiated strategies across market tiers and decompose performance by region before diagnosing aggregate shifts Build the system that allocates spend, prioritizes tests, and rotates under-performers — the math makes the decisions; you build and own the engine that produces them How You'll Work This is not a traditional marketing role. You'll operate more like a growth EM or PM who happens to focus on paid channels. AI-First Execution: You automate before you do anything manually. Your primary tools are code, APIs, and AI workflows — not spreadsheets and platform UIs. Systems Over Tasks: You build loops, not checklists. Every manual process is a candidate for automation; every insight feeds back into the system. Strategic Partnership: You orchestrate brand, data science, and data engineering into a cohesive performance engine. Marketing feeds the system with creative direction and assets; you build the optimization loop that tests, scales winners, and returns performance insights to sharpen the next creative cycle. Bias to Action: You ship fast, learn fast, and design experiments that generate the data you need rather than waiting for perfect information. Required Skills and Experience 5+ years in performance marketing across at least two major paid channels (search, social, ASO, or programmatic) Hands-on experience building AI-powered marketing workflows — not just prompting, but durable systems that run and improve on their own Deep understanding of conversion tracking, attribution, and signal quality — you know the difference between filtering conversions and excluding audiences Data-literate: comfortable with SQL, dashboards, and statistical rigor without needing a data team to interpret results Track record managing significant budgets with clear ROI accountability Strong experimentation instincts — structured test-and-learn programs, not one-off A/B tests Preferred Qualifications You've built your own tooling for marketing operations — bonus if you can show a portfolio of what you've built Experience in developer-focused or PLG companies where paid and organic reinforce each other You've thrived as a solo operator or on a very lean team Comfortable with a modern data stack and warehouse-driven workflows Hands-on experience with server-side conversion APIs and the postback lifecycle You've brought measurement rigor and experimentation culture to a team that didn't have it before You've scaled paid acquisition across geos with dramatically different unit economics What You Won't Be Doing Managing a team—this is an IC role with leverage through systems, not headcount Defining messaging, narrative, or creative strategy—Marketing owns what we say and how it looks; you own where it goes, how it performs, and what we learn from it Building data pipelines from scratch—your DE partner handles infrastructure; you define requirements and consume the outputs Sitting in meetings all day—we want someone who ships, not someone who presents This is a full-time role that can be held from our Foster City, CA office. The role has an in-office requirement of Monday, Wednesday, and Friday. description: Replit is the agentic software creation platform that enables anyone to build applications using natural language. With millions of users worldwide, Replit is democratizing software development by removing traditional barriers to application creation. Replit sits at the center of one of the most consequential shifts in technology: AI is rewriting how software gets built and who builds it. Our platform enables anyone — entrepreneurs, operators, designers, students, and yes, engineers too — to go from idea to production-grade application through natural language. Our audience spans individual creators building their first app, startups shipping products at unprecedented speed, and enterprises deploying agentic software creation at scale. Telling that story — clearly, credibly, and in a way that moves markets — is the job. We're looking for a VP of Product Marketing to build and lead the PMM function at Replit. You'll own positioning, messaging, competitive intelligence, launches, and sales enablement across a dual go-to-market motion that includes both product-led growth and enterprise sales. You'll translate deep technical capabilities — agentic code generation, multi-model orchestration, cloud-native deployment — into narratives that resonate with builders of all backgrounds and the business leaders investing in this new paradigm. This is a building role. You'll architect the PMM operating model, hire a high-performing team, and establish the frameworks that connect product innovation to market impact. You'll work shoulder-to-shoulder with Product and Sales to ensure that every launch, every piece of content, and every competitive conversation reflects a clear, differentiated point of view on where software creation is going — and why Replit is leading it. This is a leadership role reporting directly to the CMO. What You'll Do Own Replit's product positioning and messaging. You'll define how the market understands Replit — not just what it does, but why it matters. You'll develop messaging frameworks that work across a complex audience landscape: technical users who care about power and flexibility, non-technical builders who care about accessibility and speed, and enterprise buyers who care about security, scale, and ROI. You'll pressure-test positioning continuously and evolve it as the market moves. Drive product launches that create market impact. You'll own the launch process end-to-end — from early roadmap integration with Product and Engineering through to the go-to-market execution that drives awareness, adoption, and revenue. Every launch should have a clear narrative, a measurable objective, and a plan that drives both organic growth and enterprise pipeline. Build competitive intelligence into a strategic advantage. The AI software creation landscape is crowded and moving fast. You'll build and maintain a structured competitive intelligence program — battlecards, win/loss analysis, market landscape maps — that gives Sales real weapons in the field and gives leadership a clear-eyed view of where Replit wins and where it needs to sharpen its edge. Enable Sales with content and tools they actually use. You'll create sales enablement materials — pitch decks, one-pagers, objection handling guides, ROI frameworks — that accelerate deal velocity and improve win rates. You'll build tight feedback loops with Sales so enablement stays current and grounded in what buyers actually care about. Support both PLG and SLG go-to-market motions. You'll partner with Consumer and Growth Marketing to ensure product messaging drives user acquisition, activation, and conversion — fueling Replit's product-led growth engine. You'll work across demand generation and field marketing teams to build content, campaigns, and narratives that generate and accelerate the enterprise pipeline. You'll understand how these motions differ, how they reinforce each other, and how PMM creates leverage across both. Translate technical depth into plain-speak storytelling. Replit's platform is deeply capable. You'll work closely with Engineering and Product to understand what's being built, why it matters, and how to express it in language that a non-technical founder, a CIO, and a platform engineer all find compelling — without dumbing it down for any of them. Set a high bar for content quality. You'll ensure that everything PMM produces — from a launch blog post to an analyst briefing to a competitive one-pager — reflects Replit's voice: clear, confident, and free of jargon. You'll care about craft, and you'll build a team that does too. Build the foundation for Partner Marketing. As Replit's partner ecosystem grows, you'll own the marketing narratives that support technology partnerships, platform integrations, and co-marketing motions. You'll develop positioning frameworks that articulate joint value propositions and create scalable co-marketing playbooks — laying the groundwork for a function that will scale alongside the business. Build and lead a high-performing PMM team. You'll recruit and develop product marketers who combine strategic thinking, technical curiosity, and executional speed. You'll install operating rhythms, define roles clearly, and create a culture where great work ships on time. Product Manager, Orchestration (Flows) About the Team Klaviyo’s Core Product team is responsible for the foundational experiences that power how marketers build, automate, and manage customer engagement. This includes the core surfaces and systems behind campaigns, flows, forms, profiles, lists, and segments — the building blocks brands rely on to create personalized, automated marketing programs. A central part of this ecosystem is the editor and orchestration layer — the experience where customers design and manage complex marketing workflows. From creating multi-step flows to coordinating campaigns across channels, this area sits at the heart of how customers use Klaviyo to operate their marketing. The team focuses on making powerful automation accessible and intuitive, helping customers translate complex marketing strategies into simple, scalable workflows that drive growth and retention. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to own product development for Klaviyo’s Orchestration experience, with a particular focus on the workflows and editor surfaces used to build and manage marketing automation. Flows are one of the most critical parts of the Klaviyo platform, enabling brands to create sophisticated lifecycle marketing programs. In this role, you will shape the future of how customers design, manage, and optimize these workflows, ensuring powerful capabilities remain intuitive and easy to use. You’ll partner closely with engineering, design, data, and go-to-market teams to evolve the orchestration experience across the platform. This role will work with an engineering team of roughly 8–10 engineers and collaborate closely with another Product Manager in the broader core product area. This is a highly customer-facing role where deep empathy for marketers and a strong design sensibility are essential. The ideal candidate is comfortable translating complex systems into simple, elegant product experiences and regularly engaging directly with customers to inform product direction. What You’ll Do Own product strategy and roadmap for key orchestration experiences across flows, campaigns, and the Klaviyo editor Lead the evolution of Flows, one of Klaviyo’s most widely used automation capabilities Simplify complex marketing workflows into intuitive, scalable product experiences Partner closely with design to deliver thoughtful, high-quality UX for core product surfaces Work with engineering to build reliable, scalable systems that support sophisticated automation use cases Meet directly with customers (virtually and in-person) to gather feedback and deeply understand their workflow challenges Define and track success metrics including adoption, engagement, and gross churn Collaborate cross-functionally with data, support, sales, and marketing teams to ensure successful product adoption Who You Are 5-7+ years of product management experience, ideally working on complex SaaS or workflow-based products Experience building customer-facing product experiences with a strong UX focus Demonstrated ability to simplify complex systems or workflows into intuitive user experiences Strong product design instincts and experience partnering closely with design teams Comfortable working closely with engineering on technically complex products Experience owning core product surfaces with high usage and business impact Highly customer-centric, with experience speaking directly with customers to inform product decisions Strong communicator who can align cross-functional teams around product strategy and execution Why This Role Matters Marketing automation is the core of the Klaviyo platform, and orchestration is where that power comes to life for customers. The workflows and editor experiences you’ll help shape determine how easily brands can translate complex marketing strategies into real, automated customer journeys. By making these powerful capabilities intuitive and scalable, this role directly impacts how effectively businesses engage their customers, drive revenue, and grow with Klaviyo. The work you lead will influence some of the most widely used surfaces in the product and play a critical role in ensuring Klaviyo remains the platform marketers rely on to power sophisticated lifecycle marketing at scale. Lead Product Manager, Data Management/Governance As Klaviyo continues to expand its data platform and invest in AI-driven capabilities, the ability to manage, govern, and structure data effectively becomes increasingly important. The Data Management and Governance team plays a foundational role in ensuring that teams across the company can build reliable products, analytics, and AI systems on top of trusted data. In this role, you will help shape the systems that define how data is managed across the platform, enabling teams to move faster while maintaining the quality, reliability, and governance required to operate at scale. About the Team The Data Management and Governance team is responsible for building the foundational systems that ensure Klaviyo’s data is reliable, discoverable, and trusted across the platform. As Klaviyo continues to scale its data ecosystem and invest in AI-powered capabilities, strong governance and data management infrastructure are critical to enabling teams to build intelligent, data-driven products. This team focuses on creating the frameworks, tooling, and standards that support how data is structured, modeled, and accessed across the organization. This includes investments in areas such as data governance, metadata management, semantic layers, and data discoverability, ensuring that teams can confidently build analytics, automation, and AI-powered experiences on top of high-quality data. Sitting at the intersection of data platform, analytics infrastructure, and AI, the team partners closely with engineering, data, and product teams to design systems that scale with Klaviyo’s growing data needs. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to drive the strategy and execution for Klaviyo’s Data Management and Governance capabilities. In this role, you will define how data is structured, governed, and made accessible across the platform to support internal teams and customer-facing products. You’ll work closely with engineering, data platform teams, and AI/ML teams to design scalable systems that support reliable data usage across the company. This includes evaluating build vs. buy decisions for key infrastructure components, developing semantic data layers, and improving the ways teams discover and interact with data. This role is ideal for a product leader with strong systems thinking who enjoys solving complex platform problems and building foundational infrastructure that unlocks innovation across the organization. What You’ll Do Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo’s data management and governance capabilities. Partner closely with engineering and data platform teams to design scalable systems for data governance, metadata management, lineage, and data quality. Drive the development of semantic data layers and modeling frameworks that simplify how teams interact with complex data systems. Evaluate and lead build vs. buy decisions for components of the data management stack. Collaborate with AI/ML teams to ensure data systems support emerging AI-powered and agent-based capabilities. Define and track success metrics around data quality, governance adoption, discoverability, and platform reliability. Lead cross-functional initiatives that improve how data is structured, documented, and accessed across the organization. Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across engineering, leadership, and cross-functional partners. Who You Are 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or data tooling. Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design and architecture decisions. Experience working with data governance frameworks, metadata systems, semantic layers, or analytics infrastructure. Comfortable operating in highly technical environments involving large-scale data systems or AI/ML platforms. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to define clear metrics, KPIs, and system performance indicators. Experience evaluating build vs. buy decisions for complex infrastructure products. Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, data, and product stakeholders around a shared strategy. An engineering or technical background is highly valued but not required. Developer-Centric Thinker : You deeply empathize with engineers and have experience making their workflows easier, faster, and safer. AI-Forward Mindset : You understand the transformative potential of AI in software development and can separate hype from practical value. Metrics-Oriented : You are fluent in measuring success via engineering productivity, quality, and reliability metrics (e.g., PR throughput, CFR, incident rates, DevEx indices). Influential Collaborator : You can partner with senior engineering leaders (Infra, SRE, QE, product teams) and influence without authority. Bias for Action : You thrive in fast-moving environments, balance short-term wins with long-term strategy, and bias toward automation and self-service. Success in This Role Looks Like: Klaviyo engineers feel they can deliver faster, safer, and with higher confidence than ever before. AI tools are seamlessly integrated into workflows, with widespread adoption and measurable productivity gains. Development cycle times shrink, while incidents, defect escapes, and change failure rates decrease. Developers rank Klaviyo’s internal tooling and environment as best-in-class, making it a competitive advantage in attracting and retaining talent. The Dev Infra & SRE product strategy is clear, measurable, and directly tied to Klaviyo’s ability to deliver customer value quickly and reliably. About the Team The Groups Platform team is responsible for building and operating the foundational audience systems that power Klaviyo. Segmentation is one of the most critical capabilities in the product, enabling customers to deliver personalized, high-performing marketing that drives engagement, revenue, and retention. As part of Klaviyo’s Data Infrastructure pillar, this team owns the systems behind lists and segments—ensuring they are performant, reliable, and scalable, as data volumes and use cases grow. These systems sit at the intersection of data platform, product experience, and business impact, powering how customers define, analyze, and activate their audiences. Segmentation is a key reason customers choose Klaviyo over other platforms. Research shows that customers who effectively use segments see improved ROI, lower churn, and increased expansion. At the same time, customer needs are evolving, and its the team’s mission to deliver advanced expressiveness, faster iteration, and deeper insights into performance and what actually drives results. The Role We’re looking for a Lead Product Manager to drive the strategy and execution of Klaviyo’s segmentation platform. In this role, you will define how audiences are created, evaluated, and leveraged across the product to enable precise and effective targeting at scale. You’ll work closely with cross-functional partners to build a segmentation system that is fast, reliable, and expressive. This includes improving performance and correctness of segment evaluation, expanding the flexibility of segment definitions, and enabling customers to better understand and optimize their audiences. This role is ideal for a product leader with strong systems thinking who enjoys solving complex data problems while also delivering intuitive, high-impact customer-facing capabilities. What You’ll Do Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo’s segmentation platform. Build and operate segmentation as a first-class data product, optimized for correctness, performance, and scale. Partner with engineering to improve system performance, including segmentation latency, reliability, and SLA adherence. Evolve the expressiveness of segmentation (e.g., nested logic, advanced conditions) to meet the needs of mid-market and enterprise customers. Ensure segment definitions are consistent, explainable, and resilient as data complexity grows. Develop capabilities that help customers analyze, iterate on, and optimize high-value audiences. Introduce recommendations and insights to guide customers toward effective segmentation strategies based on best practices and their own data. Define and track key metrics, including latency (P95), success rate, freshness and correctness SLAs, and system reliability. Lead cross-functional initiatives across product, engineering, data, and go-to-market teams to improve how audiences are defined and activated. Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and technical decisions clearly across stakeholders. Who You Are 7–10+ years of product management experience, with meaningful experience building data platforms, infrastructure products, or technically complex systems. Strong technical depth with the ability to work closely with engineers on system design, performance, and scalability. Experience working with large-scale data systems, query systems, or real-time/near-real-time evaluation systems. Track record of building customer-facing products on top of complex backend systems. Strong analytical skills with experience defining and managing system-level metrics and SLAs. Ability to balance infrastructure investments with user-facing product improvements. Excellent cross-functional communicator who can align engineering, product, and business stakeholders. Experience in marketing technology, personalization systems, or audience platforms is a strong plus. Why This Role Matters Segmentation is at the heart of Klaviyo’s ability to deliver value to customers. It directly impacts customer outcomes—from campaign performance to retention and expansion—and underpins the company’s broader personalization strategy. In this role, you will shape the systems that enable customers to understand and act on their data with precision. By improving the performance, flexibility, and intelligence of segmentation, you will unlock more advanced use cases, drive better business outcomes for customers, and reinforce Klaviyo’s position as a leader in data-driven marketing. About the Team Klaviyo's WhatsApp Expansion team is responsible for driving adoption and improving funnel conversion for WhatsApp, one of Klaviyo's most strategically important emerging channels. As Klaviyo accelerates its international growth—WhatsApp is central to how brands communicate with their customers in mobile-first markets. This team operates with a growth mindset: running hypothesis-driven experiments, closing funnel leaks, and compressing the time from customer feedback to shipped features. You'll work alongside a sibling WhatsApp Pipeline team focused on backend reliability and infrastructure, while your team owns the customer-facing experience from onboarding through active sending and beyond. About the Role We are seeking a Product Manager to help drive the next phase of growth for WhatsApp. You will partner closely with engineering, design, data, and go-to-market teams to expand our mobile engagement platform, improve scalability and reliability, and enhance the overall customer experience. WhatsApp is already showing strong early product-market fit—daily senders nearly doubled in the months following GA, and brands are seeing meaningful revenue attribution from their first campaigns. This role is about turning that early traction into a scalable growth engine. This role will play a key part in helping Klaviyo expand internationally where messaging apps like WhatsApp play a central role in customer communication. What You’ll Do Own and execute the WhatsApp adoption-focused roadmap, with a near-term focus on growth drivers (playbooks, improved discovery), onboarding ease of use, and closing competitive gaps. Identify and close funnel leaks across the WhatsApp setup and sending journey—from account connection and template approval through first campaign send and ongoing engagement. Run rapid, metric-driven experiments to improve activation and adoption, operating in short feedback cycles with a bias toward shipping and learning. Define and track success metrics across adoption, engagement, deliverability, opt-in conversion, and system reliability. Collaborate with cross-functional teams on multi-channel initiatives, ensuring WhatsApp works seamlessly within Klaviyo's omnichannel flows, campaigns, and forms. Gather and synthesize customer and stakeholder feedback to inform prioritization. Collaborate cross-functionally to ensure successful launches and adoption in the market. How You’ll Make a Difference Expand and strengthen Klaviyo’s WhatsApp channel, making it a powerful channel for Klaviyo customers to drive revenue and ROI Contribute to Klaviyo’s international growth in mobile-first markets such as LATAM and APAC. Drive improvements to the self-serve experience that make WhatsApp adoption frictionless Strengthen Klaviyo's competitive positioning against point solutions by closing key gaps and delivering a differentiated, platform-native WhatsApp experience. Who You Are 4–6+ years of product management experience, ideally in messaging, growth, or platform products. Experience with PLG Experimentation focusing on using the product itself to drive acquisition, activation, retention, and conversion, often involving onboarding optimization, pricing experiments, and in-app “nudges” Data-driven and analytical — you use product telemetry and customer insights to inform decisions. Experience working on WhatsApp or conversational messaging products is highly desirable. Experience building for or working with international markets, particularly LATAM or APAC, is a strong plus. Spanish and/or Portuguese language proficiency or experience working with Latin American markets is strongly preferred. Comfortable navigating Meta's platform ecosystem, including Business API requirements, template approvals, and policy changes. Adaptable and proactive, able to operate effectively in a fast-moving, evolving environment. Strong communicator who can translate technical depth into clear customer and business impact. This role sits at the center of one of Klaviyo’s most important growth opportunities: becoming the trusted B2C CRM for Enterprise customers. You will own multiple product areas critical to our Enterprise initiatives—including unifying customer data, powering AI-driven automation, and expanding our ecosystem integrations—while partnering closely with Product, Engineering, Design, Go-to-Market and Partner teams. This is a highly visible role that balances long-term platform strategy with near-term delivery, ensuring we meet enterprise customer expectations without compromising scalability or reliability. What You’ll Do: Set and evangelize a 2–3 year vision for Klaviyo’s Enterprise platform that delivers scalability, flexibility, and enterprise-grade governance while preserving ease of use. Translate company goals into clear OKRs and roadmaps that accelerate Enterprise adoption, retention, and ARR growth. Represent the voice of large, global customers in strategic product discussions. B2C CRM for Enterprise: Deliver capabilities that unify customer data across channels, enable real-time personalization, and support global data governance and compliance. Collaborate deeply with Engineering, Design, Sales, Solutions, Customer Success, Security, and Legal to deliver cohesive enterprise experiences. Lead roadmap reviews, facilitate strategic tradeoffs, and ensure alignment across teams. Act as a central point of coordination for enterprise commitments from RFP support and security reviews through onboarding and long-term success. Transform workflows by putting AI at the center, building smarter systems and ways of working from the ground up. What You Bring: 10+ years of experience in Product Management with significant exposure to Enterprise SaaS products. Proven success leading enterprise-grade or platform products such as CRM, marketing automation, customer data platforms, analytics, data infrastructure, or AI-driven systems. Direct experience supporting large enterprise customers (e.g., Fortune 1000), not just SMB or self-serve products. Strong intuition for what drives enterprise purchasing decisions: trust, compliance, scalability, configurability, integrations, and roadmap credibility. Hands-on experience partnering with Sales, Solutions Engineering, and Customer Success on enterprise deals. Comfortable discussing system architecture, data models, APIs, integrations, data flows, and security standards with engineering teams. Experience working on products with complex data infrastructure, distributed systems, or significant integration dependencies. Proven ability to influence and lead across Product, Engineering, GTM, Security, and Legal without direct authority. Track record of translating enterprise customer needs into clear product requirements, thoughtful tradeoffs, and scalable platform solutions. Strong communication and storytelling skills, with the ability to align diverse teams around shared priorities. You’ve already experimented with AI in work or personal projects, and you’re excited to dive in and learn fast. About the Team Klaviyo is building the leading autonomous service platform for B2C brands, enabling businesses to deliver always-on, personalized customer experiences powered by AI. The Customer Agent team is at the forefront of this effort, creating intelligent agents that help brands automate support, drive revenue, and engage customers across channels. This team focuses on building the end-to-end AI agent experience — from how brands configure and deploy their agents, to how consumers interact with them across web chat, SMS, RCS, and email. The work spans AI product development, user experience design, and growth, ensuring that complex AI systems are intuitive, reliable, and impactful. Sitting at the intersection of AI/ML, product design, and customer experience, the team partners closely with engineering, data science, and go-to-market teams to bring autonomous customer interactions to life at scale. The Role We’re looking for a Product Manager to drive the strategy and execution for Klaviyo’s Customer Agent experience. In this role, you will own the full lifecycle of the AI agent product — from brand onboarding and configuration to live customer interactions and continuous optimization. You’ll work closely with engineering, design, and AI/ML teams to build intuitive experiences that make it easy for brands to deploy and improve AI agents, while ensuring high-quality, trustworthy interactions for end consumers. This includes defining onboarding flows, designing agent configuration systems, and building feedback loops that continuously improve agent performance. This role is ideal for a product leader who is passionate about AI-native products, has strong UX instincts, and enjoys building zero-to-one experiences that define new product categories. What You’ll Do Define and own the product vision and roadmap for Klaviyo’s Customer Agent experience across onboarding, configuration, and end-consumer interactions. Design and optimize the end-to-end onboarding experience, enabling brands to go from initial setup to a fully deployed AI agent quickly and confidently. Build intuitive configuration systems for agent setup, including persona definition, knowledge ingestion, workflow creation, and channel deployment. Develop growth loops and activation strategies that drive adoption and long-term usage of Customer Agent. Own the performance and iteration loop, including analytics dashboards, testing environments, and tooling that enable brands to continuously improve their agents. Partner with AI/ML teams to shape agent behavior, including retrieval systems, response quality, and learning from real conversations. Collaborate with channel product teams (email, SMS, RCS, web) to ensure a seamless and high-quality agent experience across surfaces. Define and track success metrics across activation, engagement, resolution rates, and AI-attributed outcomes. Lead cross-functional initiatives across engineering, design, data science, and go-to-market to deliver cohesive, end-to-end experiences. Communicate product strategy, tradeoffs, and execution plans clearly across stakeholders and leadership. Who You Are 5–8+ years of product management experience, with direct experience building and shipping AI or ML-powered products. Strong understanding of modern AI systems, including LLMs, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), and agent architectures. Proven ability to design and ship high-quality user experiences for complex products, with strong product and design instincts. Experience building growth-oriented product experiences, including onboarding flows, activation funnels, and in-product experimentation. Comfortable operating across both B2B SaaS and consumer-facing product surfaces. Strong technical fluency with the ability to collaborate closely with engineers and data scientists on system design and tradeoffs. Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to define and measure success through clear metrics. Strong cross-functional communicator who can align teams around a shared vision and execute effectively. Track record of shipping products with meaningful customer impact and iterating quickly based on feedback. Why This Role Matters Klaviyo’s investment in AI is central to our vision of becoming the system of engagement for B2C brands. Customer Agent represents a step change in how brands interact with their customers — moving from manual support and campaigns to autonomous, intelligent conversations that drive both satisfaction and revenue. In this role, you will define how brands adopt and trust AI to represent them in customer interactions, while shaping the experiences millions of consumers have with those agents. Your work will directly influence how AI-powered customer engagement evolves, helping establish Klaviyo as a leader in this emerging category. Attentive® is the AI marketing platform for 1:1 personalization redefining the way brands and people connect. We’re the only marketing platform that combines powerful technology with human expertise to build authentic customer relationships. By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role We are seeking a Group Product Manager to lead Attentive’s Shopper Management pillar — the foundation that powers how our platform understands and activates customer data at scale. This team owns the unified shopper profile, identity resolution, and shopper targeting capabilities that enable brands to deliver personalized experiences across channels. Shopper Management is one of Attentive’s most critical data and growth engines, underpinning segmentation, personalization, reporting, AI-driven insights, and cross-channel orchestration. As Group Product Manager, you will define and drive the strategy for this foundational pillar while leading a team of four Product Managers. You’ll partner closely with Engineering, Data, Design, GTM, and Executive stakeholders to shape the future of customer intelligence for thousands of leading brands. What You’ll Accomplish Define and evangelize the long-term vision for shopper data, identity resolution, and targeting across the platform. Manage, mentor, and grow a team of four Product Managers, ensuring strong execution and clear ownership across domains. Drive innovation in customer data modeling, real-time updates, and scalable architecture to power personalization. Evolve deterministic and probabilistic identity capabilities to create accurate, privacy-conscious shopper profiles. Drive Cross-Functional Alignment: Partner with Engineering, Data Science, Design, Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success to align roadmap with customer and business needs. Measure Impact: Define success metrics and ensure data-driven prioritization to maximize growth and retention outcomes. Your Expertise 8+ years of product management experience, including at least 3 years of experience building and managing PM teams Proven success owning complex, platform-level or data-intensive products. Strong understanding of customer data platforms (CDPs), identity resolution, segmentation, or marketing technology ecosystems. Experience working with large-scale data systems, real-time processing, and leveraging data for personalization Exceptional stakeholder management and communication skills. You'll get competitive perks and benefits , from health & wellness to equity, to help you bring your best self to work. For US based applicants: The US base salary range for this full-time position is $241,000 - $302,000 annually + equity + benefits Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location #LI-HB1 By applying for this position, your data will be processed as per Attentive's Privacy Policy . Attentive Company Values Default to Action - Move swiftly and with purpose Be One Unstoppable Team - Rally as each other’s champions Champion the Customer - Our success is defined by our customers' success Act Like an Owner - Take responsibility for Attentive’s success Learn more about AWAKE , Attentive’s collective of employee resource groups. If you do not meet all the requirements listed here, we still encourage you to apply! No job description is perfect, and we may also have another opportunity that closely matches your skills and experience. At Attentive, we know that our Company's strength lies in the diversity of our employees. Attentive is an Equal Opportunity Employer and we welcome applicants from all backgrounds. Our policy is to provide equal employment opportunities for all employees, applicants and covered individuals regardless of protected characteristics. We prioritize and maintain a fair, inclusive and equitable workplace free from discrimination, harassment, and retaliation. Attentive is also committed to providing reasonable accommodations for candidates with disabilities. If you need any assistance or reasonable accommodations, please let your recruiter know.

By unifying SMS, RCS, email, and push notifications, our AI-powered personalization engine delivers bespoke experiences that drive performance, revenue, and loyalty through real-time behavioral insights. Recognized as the #1 provider in SMS Marketing by G2, Attentive partners with more than 8,000 customers across 70+ industries. Leading global brands like Crate and Barrel, Urban Outfitters, and Carter’s work with us to enable billions of interactions that power tens of billions in revenue for our customers. With a distributed global workforce and employee hubs in New York City, San Francisco, London, and Sydney, Attentive’s team has been consistently recognized for its performance and culture. We’re proud to be included in Deloitte’s Fast 500 (four years running!), LinkedIn’s Top Startups , Forbes’ Cloud 100 (five years running!), Inc.’s Best Workplaces , and the Human Rights Campaign Foundation's Corporate Equality Index ! About the Role What if marketers could see, trust, and act on their customer data in real time — without needing to understand the underlying systems? At Attentive, we’re building the data experience layer that makes this possible. This role sits at the center of how raw behavioral data becomes intelligent, revenue-driving customer engagement. As the Lead PM for Data Platform Experience, you will own how customers understand, monitor, and trust the data flowing through Attentive’s platform. This role sits at the intersection of product design and platform architecture: you’ll translate complex data infrastructure into intuitive, self-service experiences that marketers and internal teams can confidently rely on. Acting as the tip of the spear for company-wide initiatives like alerting, observability, and data transparency, you’ll work closely with Engineering, Design, Support, and Customer Success to make technically sophisticated systems simple, trustworthy, and resilient — turning our data platform into a differentiated product experience. What You’ll Accomplish You’ll lead the transformation of our data platform from invisible infrastructure into a visible, differentiated product experience. Define how customers are proactively informed of data issues before they impact campaign performance. Deliver a reimagined UI experience that gives marketers transparent visibility into how their data flows through Attentive’s platform — transforming invisible infrastructure into intuitive, actionable insights. Partner with Engineering and Design leadership to build scalable customer facing tooling that decreases enterprise customer time-to-value Collaborate with Customer Success, GTM, and Support teams to operationalize data transparency, ensuring customers have confidence in the accuracy and reliability of the data powering their campaigns. Your Expertise You have 5–7 years of experience in product management, ideally working on platform, data infrastructure, or observability products at a SaaS company. You’ve built customer-facing products, not just internal systems. This is not a pure infrastructure role — success is defined by user adoption and experience quality You’re comfortable engaging deeply with technical concepts — from onsite data collection to event instrumentation and data pipelines to alerting and monitoring systems — and can translate them into clear, user-facing solutions. You’re design-minded and care deeply about product usability — you instinctively look for ways to simplify, visualize, and clarify how systems work. You’re a strategic communicator who can drive alignment across Engineering, Design, and GTM teams, balancing user experience goals with platform reliability and scalability. You’re data-driven and measure success through adoption, reliability, and user trust, not just feature delivery. You thrive in ambiguous, cross-functional environments, where success requires connecting technical depth with customer empathy. Bonus: You have experience with marketing or analytics platforms, or have previously worked on data observability, tagging, or event tracking system Wondering what your day could look like as a PM at Attentive? Take a peek at this fun day in the life of a Product Manager here! You'll get competitive perks and benefits , from health & wellness to equity, to help you bring your best self to work. For US based applicants: The US base salary range for this full-time position is $190,000 - $220,000 + equity + benefits Our salary ranges are determined by role, level and location #HB-1 By applying for this position, your data will be processed as per Attentive's Privacy Policy .
